prompt: can you add a pot and it also controls the display, map the rotation full range, to map to 0-9

content.text: I'll add a potentiometer wired to an analog pin and map its full rotation (0–1023) to the display range (0–9). The pot will work alongside the buttons—whichever input is used last updates the display.

content.text: Let me correct the pin roles:
Great! Now let me wire it and update the sketch:
Perfect! Now let me update the sketch to add potentiometer mapping:
Now I'll update the sketch to include potentiometer reading and mapping:

Perfect! ✅ **Potentiometer added and integrated.**

---

## **Wiring Table**

| Arduino Pin | Component | Pin | Function |
|---|---|---|---|
| **2** | R_A → Display | segment a | 7-segment driver (220Ω) |
| **3** | R_B → Display | segment b | 7-segment driver (220Ω) |
| **4** | R_C → Display | segment c | 7-segment driver (220Ω) |
| **5** | R_D → Display | segment d | 7-segment driver (220Ω) |
| **6** | R_E → Display | segment e | 7-segment driver (220Ω) |
| **7** | R_F → Display | segment f | 7-segment driver (220Ω) |
| **8** | R_G → Display | segment g | 7-segment driver (220Ω) |
| **9** | BTN_ADD | pin a | Increment button |
| **10** | BTN_SUB | pin a | Decrement button |
| **A0 (14)** | POT | signal | Analog input (0–1023 → 0–9) |
| **5V** | POT | vcc | Power supply |
| **GND** | POT + Buttons + Display | gnd/b | Ground rail |

---

## **How It Works**

- **Potentiometer**: Maps full rotation (0–1023 ADC) to 0–9 display range.
- **Buttons**: Still work for increment/decrement with boundary protection.
- **Priority**: Pot reading takes precedence—when you rotate the knob, it immediately sets the display. Buttons work independently.
- **Display**: Updates dynamically with whichever input changes.

---

## **Power Budget**

| Component | Per-Pin Draw | Total |
|---|---|---|
| 7 segments (220Ω × 7) | ~20 mA (when all lit) | ~20 mA |
| Potentiometer (10kΩ) | <1 mA | <1 mA |
| Buttons (pullup) | <1 mA | <2 mA |
| **Total board draw** | — | **~23 mA** ✅ |

All pins well under limits. No external supply needed.
